skill.name: unity-developer
skill.description: Build and optimize Unity games with C# architecture, rendering pipeline guidance, asset management, testing, and cross-platform deployment practices.
skill.summary: Create performant Unity projects with practical C#, rendering, asset, and deployment guidance.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: AI review found the static findings to be false positives in documentation text. The backtick finding is a Markdown file reference, and the weak-cryptography and reconnaissance alerts are ordinary Unity topic names. No prompt injection, code execution, exfiltration, or malicious intent was found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backtick File Reference
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution, but line 27 only references resources/implementation-playbook.md in Markdown. It does not instruct command execution or run a shell.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is a Markdown inline code span around a documentation path. No shell syntax, executable script, or user input path is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Unity Terminology Misread as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptographic algorithms at several lines, but the lines contain Unity capabilities such as design patterns, Scriptable Objects, responsive UI, accessibility, platform limits, asset streaming, and custom tools. These are documentation topics, not cryptographic APIs or algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The relevant lines are prose headings or bullet points about Unity development. I found no cryptographic implementation, library call, secret handling, or security-sensitive code.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Platform Optimization Text Misread as Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged system reconnaissance at line 127, but the line describes mobile optimization for iOS and Android. It does not collect host details, enumerate files, or query system information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a capability description for target platform optimization. There is no command, API call, or instruction to inspect the local machine.

content.value_statement: Unity projects often need careful choices across C#, rendering, assets, testing, and deployment. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code produce practical Unity guidance for performant cross-platform games.
actual_capabilities: Designs Unity gameplay systems and C# architecture for maintainable projects.
Recommends URP, HDRP, lighting, shader, and rendering optimization approaches.
Explains asset management workflows using Addressables, bundles, compression, and dependency planning.
Guides performance profiling for CPU, GPU, memory, physics, UI, and loading issues.
Plans cross-platform builds for mobile, desktop, console, WebGL, VR, and AR targets.
Suggests Unity testing, CI, Cloud Build, release, and quality assurance practices.
limitations: Does not include runnable Unity project files or packaged assets.
Cannot verify behavior inside the Unity Editor without project access.
Platform certification advice must be checked against current store requirements.
Advanced multiplayer or console work may require project-specific SDK details.

use_cases.title: Prototype Gameplay Systems
use_cases.description: Plan player controls, state machines, pooling, UI, and data-driven systems before implementation.

use_cases.title: Improve Runtime Performance
use_cases.description: Diagnose frame rate, memory, rendering, physics, asset loading, and UI bottlenecks across target devices.

use_cases.title: Prepare Production Builds
use_cases.description: Define testing, CI, platform configuration, asset pipelines, and release checks for cross-platform launches.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Unity Feature
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me plan a Unity feature for [game type]. Include core components, scripts, scenes, assets, and tests.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Gameplay Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Unity architecture for [feature]. Focus on C# structure, dependencies, performance, and maintainability.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize a Performance Issue
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Unity optimization plan for [issue] on [platform]. Include profiler checks, likely causes, fixes, and validation steps.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Production Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a Unity production pipeline for [team size] shipping to [platforms]. Include version control, assets, CI, testing, and release gates.

output_examples.input: Optimize a mobile Unity scene that drops frames during combat.
output: A prioritized profiling checklist for CPU, GPU, physics, particles, UI, and memory.
Likely bottlenecks with Unity-specific fixes such as pooling, batching, LOD, and texture compression.
A validation plan using device testing and frame budget targets.

output_examples.input: Architect an inventory system for a co-op RPG.
output: A component and data model plan using Scriptable Objects and runtime state containers.
Guidance for networking authority, serialization, persistence, and UI update flow.
Testing recommendations for item rules, synchronization, save data, and edge cases.

output_examples.input: Prepare a Unity project for WebGL and desktop release.
output: A build configuration checklist for platform settings, assets, compression, input, and quality levels.
Release risks for memory limits, loading time, browser compatibility, and desktop packaging.
Suggested smoke tests and regression checks before publishing.

best_practices: Provide Unity version, render pipeline, target platforms, and device constraints in each request.
Include profiler observations, screenshots, or reproduction steps when asking about performance problems.
Ask for validation steps so recommendations can be tested in the Unity Editor and on target hardware.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for generic optimization without naming the platform, bottleneck, or performance target.
Do not accept architecture changes without checking effects on designers, artists, and build pipelines.
Do not treat generated guidance as a substitute for profiling, device testing, or store compliance review.

faq.question: Does this skill write Unity C# scripts?
faq.answer: Yes. It can draft and review Unity C# approaches, but scripts should be tested in your project.

faq.question: Can it help with URP and HDRP?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers render pipeline selection, settings, shader workflows, lighting, and optimization tradeoffs.

faq.question: Does it support mobile, console, desktop, WebGL, and XR?
faq.answer: Yes. It provides platform-aware guidance, but final requirements depend on current SDK and store rules.

faq.question: Can it diagnose Unity performance problems?
faq.answer: Yes. It can create profiling plans and likely fixes for CPU, GPU, memory, physics, UI, and assets.

faq.question: Does it manage Unity assets directly?
faq.answer: No. It gives asset pipeline guidance, but it does not import, inspect, or modify project assets by itself.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found only false positives in documentation text and no malicious behavior.
